meaning: vi.消失,突然不见,[数]成为零n.[语]弱化音
examples: Ann saw the cheer vanish from her children face.
The track seemed to vanish a hundred yards from where I stood.
Some people believe that schools and libraries will vanish in the wake of the Internet.
But then, all too often, the owners vanish when the company runs into debt.
An enemy that could come out of nowhere and then vanish just as fast.
